integration_gap: The current zpe-motion-kernel package implements the .zpbot encode/decode path in pure Python via src/zpe_robotics/codec.py and src/zpe_robotics/wire.py, and its pyproject.toml exposes no Rust build or binding dependency. ZPE-IMC does contain a real PyO3 extension, but that extension serves the multimodal zpe_imc_kernel stream contract through code/zpe_multimodal/core/imc_native.py and code/rust/imc_kernel/src/lib.rs rather than a robotics .zpbot packet ABI. There is no existing encode_packet/decode_packet binding for the robotics packet surface to wire into today.
recommended_integration_path: Do not claim current Rust integration. If Phase 9 later proves a real need for a robotics-native path, add a dedicated PyO3 robotics packet ABI that exposes encode_packet and decode_packet for the frozen .zpbot contract and consume it from zpe-motion-kernel behind the existing Python API with a single-command pip install path. Until that ABI exists, treat robotics Rust routing as a separate engineering task rather than a broken configuration toggle.
